mission.title: fluent-bit: Fluent-bit container crashes with exit code 136 on k8s logging to AWS ES
mission.description: Fluent-bit container crashes with exit code 136 on k8s logging to AWS ES . This issue affects 9+ users.

steps.title: Identify fluent-bit troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your fluent-bit deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit -n fluent-bit --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review fluent-bit configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant fluent-bit configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl get configmap -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=fluent-bit
```
I'm new to fluent-bit and using the the provided example yaml files with k8s 1.19.12 and AWS ES 7.9.  No customi

steps.title: Apply the fix for Fluent-bit container crashes with exit code 136 on k8s…
steps.description:
I modified re-allocation logic #3788 
The patch tries to divide by `converted_size`.
If `converted_size` is 0, it causes SIGFPE and fluent-bit exits code 136.

----
Enter `[N/A]` in the box, if an item is not applicable to your change.

Before we can approve your change; please submit the following
```yaml
[INPUT]
    Name tail
    Path a.log
    Read_From_Head true

[OUTPUT]
    Name es
    Match *
```

steps.title: Confirm Fluent-bit container crashes with exit code 136… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit -n fluent-bit --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n fluent-bit --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
I modified re-allocation logic #3788 
The patch tries to divide by `converted_size`.
If `converted_size` is 0, it causes SIGFPE and fluent-bit exits code 136.

----
Enter `[N/A]` in the box, if an item is not applicable to your change.
